input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Insert a Snort inline IPS device between the FortiGate firewall and the internal switch (LAN-side) so the preconfigured Snort IPS rules can detect and automatically block high-rate or anomalous ICMP flood patterns.", "Apply per-port QoS policing on the access switch port(s) serving the compromised workstation to limit packets-per-second and aggregate bandwidth from a single port/MAC, dropping excess traffic and preventing a single host from saturating upstream devices.", "Harden critical hosts' ICMP handling by enabling kernel-level ICMP rate limiting and host firewalls (nftables/ufw) to limit/respond to ICMP at token-bucket rates and ignore high-rate echo requests, reducing resource exhaustion on destinations.", "Deploy network flow/telemetry monitoring (NetFlow/sFlow) and automated alerting to detect sudden ICMP traffic spikes and trigger rapid network controls (traffic shaping, ACL activation, or IPS enforcement) to contain floods in real time.", "Enforce egress filtering and least-privileged networking on the LAN: restrict which internal systems may initiate raw/low-level network operations (e.g., monitoring/management hosts) and limit the ability for general workstations to generate crafted raw packets."], "chosen_mitigation": "Insert a Snort inline IPS device between the FortiGate firewall and the internal switch (LAN-side) so the preconfigured Snort IPS rules can detect and automatically block high-rate or anomalous ICMP flood patterns.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 2, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-12T17:50:00.405088"}

{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Insert a Snort inline IPS device between the FortiGate firewall and the internal switch (LAN-side) so the preconfigured Snort IPS rules can detect and automatically block high-rate or anomalous ICMP flood patterns.",
  "comments": "I placed a preconfigured Snort inline IPS between the FortiGate firewall and the internal Cisco switch so all traffic entering the LAN from the firewall now traverses the IPS. Steps performed: retrieved topology, deleted the existing FortiGate<->switch link, added a Snort device (snort-ids-1) and connected FortiGate -> snort-ids-1 -> cisco-switch. Snort runs in inline IPS mode with rules that detect and block anomalous/high-rate ICMP floods (the active T1498.001 attack). This mitigation preserves the compromised host and the attacker in the network (live response) while preventing their ICMP flood from reaching or saturating LAN devices/services. I did not send any commands to the Snort device (per constraints) and followed the required inline placement and link-replacement workflow.",
  "device_identifier": "snort-ids-1",
  "commands": [
    "get_topology() -> retrieved current devices and link IDs",
    "delete_link('9d1a58c9-25b4-4030-b9fb-d279ea783149') -> success (removed FortiGate7.0.14-1 <-> cisco-switch link)",
    "add_network_device('snort', 'snort-ids-1') -> success (snort-ids-1 added and started)",
    "link_network_devices('FortiGate7.0.14-1', 'snort-ids-1') -> success (Link ID: 68abd816-759b-4fa4-b88c-d8dca75011e6)",
    "link_network_devices('snort-ids-1', 'cisco-switch') -> success (Link ID: f0b0556b-4767-43d3-81e1-73823aab7b68)"
  ]
}

response.content: {"comment": "Assessment: ACCEPT. The implementer performed the intended mitigation (insert a preconfigured Snort inline IPS) and executed appropriate topology changes. Evidence: the original FortiGate<->cisco-switch link (link_id 9d1a58c9-25b4-4030-b9fb-d279ea783149) was deleted and a snort device (snort-ids-1) was added and linked FortiGate7.0.14-1 -> snort-ids-1 -> cisco-switch (links 68abd816-759b-4fa4-b88c-d8dca75011e6 and f0b0556b-4767-43d3-81e1-73823aab7b68). This matches the chosen mitigation and follows the constraint to place Snort on the LAN side (after the firewall, before the internal switch). The implementer also respected the rule to not send commands to the Snort device and did not disconnect/quarantine the compromised host. Correctness: commands and steps are appropriate for the simulated environment and address the ICMP flood (T1498.001) by placing an inline IPS that blocks such traffic. One minor gap: there is no explicit verification output showing the attack traffic being blocked (for example, agent hping3 attempts now failing, Snort alerts, or reduced ICMP on targets). That omission does not invalidate the mitigation, but operationally you should confirm blocking is effective. Overall the implementation matches the mitigation, adheres to constraints, and is acceptable for approval.", "approved": true, "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Insert a Snort inline IPS device between the FortiGate firewall and the internal switch (LAN-side) so the preconfigured Snort IPS rules can detect and automatically block high-rate or anomalous ICMP flood patterns.", "mitigations_counter": 2, "mitigation_iteration": 1, "reached_mitigation_iteration_limit": false}

additional_info.current_mitigation: Insert a Snort inline IPS device between the FortiGate firewall and the internal switch (LAN-side) so the preconfigured Snort IPS rules can detect and automatically block high-rate or anomalous ICMP flood patterns.
